g.data-narration: Q-Learning is a powerful model-free reinforcement learning algorithm that learns optimal decision-making policies.
g.text: Q-Learning: A Model-Free Reinforcement Learning Algorithm

g.data-narration: Q-Learning learns the optimal policy without requiring a model of the environment, using temporal difference updates.
g.text: A model-free, off-policy RL algorithm that learns optimal policy in MDPs.
Updates action-value function based on Temporal Difference error.
Can learn from experiences without following deployment policy.

g.data-narration: This algorithm powers game-playing AI, robotics, and autonomous systems by learning from interaction alone.
g.text: • Powers game-playing AI (Chess, Go, Atari games)
• Enables autonomous robots to learn from trial and error
• Foundation for modern reinforcement learning research

g.data-narration: Q-Learning operates within a Markov Decision Process, where an agent makes decisions through states and actions.
The Q-function estimates the expected cumulative reward for taking an action in a specific state.
g.text: Q(s,a) = Expected return when taking action a in state s
Represents the quality of taking action a in state s - higher Q-value means better action.

g.data-narration: The core Q-learning update uses the TD error to progressively improve Q-value estimates toward optimality.
g.text: Q(s,a) ← Q(s,a) + α [ r + γ · max Q(s',a') - Q(s,a) ]

g.data-narration: The learning rate α controls update magnitude, while discount factor γ balances immediate versus future rewards.
Q-Learning iterates through initialize-choose action-observe-update steps until Q-values converge to optimality.
g.text: Initialize Q arbitrarily (often zeros). Use ε-greedy for action selection.
Take action a, observe reward r and next state s'. Update using the Q-learning formula.

g.data-narration: In a grid world, an agent learns to navigate from start to goal, receiving rewards for each step taken.
The ε-greedy strategy balances exploration of new actions with exploitation of known good actions.
Q-Learning is off-policy, learning optimal values while following a behavior policy, unlike SARSA which learns on-policy.
g.text: Key: Q-Learning uses max(Q(s',a')) for update → optimal; SARSA uses Q(s',a') from policy → safe

g.data-narration: Q-Learning is model-free and off-policy, making it flexible and widely applicable across many domains.
g.text: Can learn from any experience, even random actions
Easy to implement, proven convergence, most popular RL algorithm

g.data-narration: Q-Learning faces challenges with large state spaces and slow convergence in complex environments.
g.text: Can require many episodes in complex environments

g.data-narration: Convergence requires appropriate learning rate and infinite visits to each state-action pair under proper conditions.
g.text: Q-Learning converges to optimal Q* under conditions:
• Learning rate α ∈ (0,1] satisfying Σαₜ = ∞ and Σαₜ² < ∞
• Infinite visits to each state-action pair

g.data-narration: Q-Learning is essential for modern RL, enabling agents to learn optimal policies through simple temporal difference updates.
g.text: • Q-Learning: Model-free, off-policy algorithm for optimal policy learning
• Update: Q(s,a) ← Q(s,a) + α[r + γmax Q(s',a') - Q(s,a)]
